original_hypothesis.title: Missing Upper Price Range Validation in initILOPool Allows Invalid Liquidity Position Creation
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ILOManager.initILOPool only validates `sqrtRatioLowerX96 < _project.initialPoolPriceX96` but does NOT validate `_project.initialPoolPriceX96 < sqrtRatioUpperX96`. If initialPoolPriceX96 >= sqrtRatioUpperX96, the concentrated liquidity position is entirely in one token. The buy() and launch() functions use _saleAmountNeeded which calculates liquidity based on the assumption that price is within range, producing incorrect values. Investors purchase positions with miscalculated liquidity, and launch may fail or deploy incorrect token ratios.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Valid concentrated liquidity requires the current price to be strictly within the tick range. The RANGE check in initILOPool is incomplete—it only enforces the lower bound. This is a clear missing validation in the contract code.

proof_of_signal.pattern: require(sqrtRatioLowerX96 < _project.initialPoolPriceX96 && sqrtRatioLowerX96 < sqrtRatioUpperX96, 'RANGE') — missing initialPoolPriceX96 < sqrtRatioUpperX96

preconditions: Project admin creates ILO pool with tickUpper such that sqrtRatioUpperX96 <= initialPoolPriceX96
The RANGE require check passes because lower < initial AND lower < upper

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Only lower bound of price range is validated
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: If price >= upper tick, getLiquidityForAmount0/1 in _saleAmountNeeded produces incorrect liquidity calculations

analysis_questions: What exact values does getLiquidityForAmount0/1 return when price >= upper tick?
Does the internal swap of A,B in LiquidityAmounts produce a valid but wrong result?
Can launch succeed with these wrong values, or does it revert?
validation_requirements: Create ILO pool with price at or above upper tick, verify RANGE check passes
Attempt buy() and verify liquidity calculations are incorrect

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Project admin (malicious or mistaken) sets tickUpper such that sqrtRatioUpperX96 < initialPoolPriceX96. ILO pool is created. Investors buy positions with incorrectly calculated liquidity. At launch, addLiquidity deploys with wrong token ratios, potentially requiring more SALE_TOKEN than available (revert) or deploying an under-collateralized position.
prerequisites: Project admin sets invalid tick parameters
Investors deposit RAISE_TOKEN into the pool

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Investors receive positions with incorrect liquidity amounts; launch may fail permanently locking funds
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All investors in the affected ILO pool
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Refund path exists if launch fails, but requires waiting for refundDeadline

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for price at or above upper tick boundary

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable by verifying price range parameters at pool creation

validation_result.mechanism: missing upper bound price range validation
validation_result.impact_summary: initILOPool allows creation of ILO pools where initialPoolPriceX96 >= sqrtRatioUpperX96, causing incorrect liquidity calculations in buy() and likely launch failure, potentially locking investor funds until refundDeadline.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The RANGE check at ILOManager.sol line 74 validates `sqrtRatioLowerX96 < _project.initialPoolPriceX96 && sqrtRatioLowerX96 < sqrtRatioUpperX96` but omits the critical check `_project.initialPoolPriceX96 < sqrtRatioUpperX96`. This allows a project admin (malicious or mistaken) to create an ILO pool where the initial price is at or above the upper tick boundary. In Uniswap V3, when the current price exceeds the upper tick of a position, the position is entirely in token0 and requires zero token1. The _saleAmountNeeded and buy() functions use LiquidityAmounts.getLiquidityForAmount0/1 which internally swap arguments when A > B, producing mathematically valid but semantically incorrect liquidity values for the intended price range. At launch, when Uniswap V3's mint() is called, it calculates actual token amounts needed based on the real price-to-tick relationship. If RAISE_TOKEN is the token that Uniswap doesn't need (because price is outside range), the amountMin slippage check will fail (0 < totalRaised), reverting launch permanently. Even if launch somehow proceeds, the liquidity math is wrong, leading to incorrect token deployment.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal is accurate. ILOManager.sol line 74 contains `require(sqrtRatioLowerX96 < _project.initialPoolPriceX96 && sqrtRatioLowerX96 < sqrtRatioUpperX96, "RANGE")` which checks lower < initial and lower < upper, but NOT initial < upper. This is a clear missing validation. The downstream effects in _saleAmountNeeded (ILOPool.sol lines ~295-310) and buy() (ILOPool.sol lines ~119-123) are confirmed through code tracing. LiquidityAmounts.getLiquidityForAmount0/1 auto-swap when A > B, masking the invalid input with a wrong but non-reverting result.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Missing check: _project.initialPoolPriceX96 < sqrtRatioUpperX96. Allows pool creation where initial price >= upper tick boundary, which makes the concentrated liquidity position entirely one-sided and breaks the raise-to-liquidity math.

When SQRT_RATIO_X96 >= SQRT_RATIO_UPPER_X96, the getLiquidityForAmount0/1 functions auto-swap their arguments, computing liquidity for an inverted range. This produces incorrect liquidity and saleAmountNeeded values.
Same issue as _saleAmountNeeded: when price >= upper tick, the liquidity calculation uses auto-swapped parameters, assigning wrong liquidity amounts to investor positions.
The auto-swap of A and B means the function silently accepts invalid inputs where the current price exceeds the range boundary, returning a non-reverting but semantically wrong liquidity value.

calling_sequence: 1. Project admin calls initILOPool with tickUpper where getSqrtRatioAtTick(tickUpper) <= initialPoolPriceX96
2. ILOManager.sol:72-74: sqrtRatioLowerX96 and sqrtRatioUpperX96 computed from ticks. RANGE check passes because lower < initial AND lower < upper
4. ILOPool.initialize:80 calls _saleAmountNeeded(params.hardCap) with wrong ratio relationships, producing incorrect maxSaleAmount
5. Investor calls buy(): ILOPool.sol:119-123 computes liquidityDelta with getLiquidityForAmount0/1 using swapped price arguments, assigning wrong liquidity to position
6. At launch: ILOPool.launch() calls _saleAmountNeeded(totalRaised) with wrong ratios, then addLiquidity() calls Uniswap pool.mint()
7. Uniswap V3 mint() computes actual amounts based on real price vs tick relationship: when price > upper tick, one token amount is 0
8. Slippage check fails (amount0Min or amount1Min = totalRaised but actual returned = 0 for one token), launch reverts
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: ILOManager.initILOPool validates RANGE at line 74 with only two conditions instead of three. The missing condition (initialPoolPriceX96 < sqrtRatioUpperX96) allows pool creation with invalid parameters. This propagates through ILOPool.initialize (stores invalid state), buy() (computes wrong liquidity per investor), _saleAmountNeeded() (computes wrong sale amounts), and launch() (fails at Uniswap mint slippage check or deploys wrong amounts).

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts configure tick ranges or pool prices - these are set by project admins at runtime via initProject/initILOPool.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: A project admin (malicious or accidentally) creates an ILO pool where tickUpper results in sqrtRatioUpperX96 <= initialPoolPriceX96. Example: initialPoolPriceX96 = 300, sqrtRatioLowerX96 = 100, sqrtRatioUpperX96 = 200. The RANGE check (100 < 300 && 100 < 200) passes. Investors deposit RAISE_TOKEN via buy(), receiving positions with incorrectly calculated liquidity. At launch, the Uniswap V3 pool requires 0 of one token (because price is outside range), but amountMin is set to totalRaised for that token, causing the slippage check to revert. Launch permanently fails. Investors must wait until refundDeadline to recover funds.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. The missing validation allows creating permanently broken ILO pools. Investors who deposit funds face: (1) launch failure requiring wait until refundDeadline, (2) if a malicious admin combines this with the owner extending refundDeadline, funds could be locked indefinitely. Even in the benign case, this is a functional bug that wastes gas and delays fund recovery.
recommended_mitigations: Add the missing upper bound check: require(sqrtRatioLowerX96 < _project.initialPoolPriceX96 && _project.initialPoolPriceX96 < sqrtRatioUpperX96, "RANGE");
Consider also validating the tick range in ILOPool.initialize as a defense-in-depth measure
Add unit tests for boundary cases where initial price equals or exceeds the upper tick
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy ILOManager and ILOPool implementation. 2. Call initProject with initialPoolPriceX96 set to a high value. 3. Call initILOPool with tickUpper corresponding to sqrtRatioUpperX96 < initialPoolPriceX96. 4. Verify RANGE check passes. 5. Call buy() and verify liquidityDelta is computed from swapped price arguments. 6. Attempt launch() and verify it reverts at the slippage check in addLiquidity.
uncertainties: The exact revert behavior at launch depends on which token is RAISE_TOKEN vs SALE_TOKEN and the specific tick values chosen
In some edge cases where sqrtRatioUpperX96 is very close to initialPoolPriceX96, the liquidity math may produce values that appear valid but are economically wrong
additional_testing_needed: Test with RAISE_TOKEN as token0 and token1 separately with price above upper tick
Test with price exactly equal to sqrtRatioUpperX96 (boundary case)
Test whether any combination allows launch to succeed with incorrect liquidity deployment
